Tandem, new free OpenClaw AI browser. So, Tandem browser just dropped and it changes everything about how you use Open Claw. Most people are using OpenClaw with a normal browser, but that's like strapping a jet engine to a shopping cart. Robin Wlander just released the public beta of Tandem browser. You can see it right here in action. And this is the first browser ever built from scratch specifically for openclaw and it's completely free. Let me show you exactly what this is, why it matters, and how to set it up today. So if you want to set this up, what you're going to do is you're going to grab the GitHub URL for this page right here, Tandem Browser. And what you can do from here is you can paste this directly into Tandem browser and open claw. Right? So it said Tandem browser is up and running. took about 5 minutes to set up and now it's running so that it can just browse the internet from openclaw directly. So the cool thing about this is basically I can use my open claw inside this extra browser and it's kind of like just gives me more autonomy plus I can use them side by side. So, you can see OpenClaw on the left hand side, which is operating tandem and the C-pilot AI agent browser over here, right? And so, now you have an autonomous AI agent that can run locally for free and it can control and scroll the web as well directly. Right? So, I'm going to say and you can see it's using the tool right now to run this directly. In the meantime, let's talk about what is Tandem browser. So, Tandem is a brand new browser, but it's not like Chrome or Firefox. It was built from day one so that you and your AI agent can browse the web together. Think of it like this. You sit in the driver's seat, open sits in the passenger seat, you both look at the same screen, you both take the wheel. That's stand up, right? And so it's an agentic browser built as an openclaw first browser environment. And so it can basically just go off and start looking across the web. So if we say okay, go inside tandem and search latest AI news. There's a little demo. You can see that it's now looking and googling the latest AI news, right? So, we gave it that command inside OpenClaw and now it's controlling our browser over here, which is pretty powerful. I've never seen that before and I've tested OpenClaw a lot with many different automations. Now, you could previously use something like browser automation and you could previously use Chrome as well, but the thing is you might want like a totally separate instance and also like the AI agent, it struggles inside Chrome. I've tested it a couple of times and sometimes it doesn't do everything correctly. If you can have a browser that's designed for openclaw first, then obviously you're going to get a much better experience, right? Get better results and it's going to work a lot more seamlessly. Like you can see it's working over here, right? It just works with the tool selection and that sort of thing. It can navigate the web. It can click through pages as it's doing right now. And so it's a lot more powerful now. Right now when OpenClaw uses a browser, it's basically going to into Chrome from the outside, right? With tandem, it's basically designed [snorts] for open claw, right? So, it can control tabs, it can take screenshots, it can look at the content on the page, it can manage sessions, it can use different tools and it's all through one local connection, right? So, it's really really cool in terms of the way it looks and how it works etc. Right?
